title: Kapiolani
content:
ย ย ย ย Where the great green combers break in thunder on the barrier reefs,--
ย ย ย ย Where, unceasing, sounds the mighty diapason of the deep,--
ย ย ย ย Ringed in bursts of wild wave-laughter, ringed in leagues of flying foam,--
ย ย ย ย Long lagoons of softest azure, curving beaches white as snow,
ย ย ย ย Lap in sweetness and in beauty all the isles of Owhyhee.

ย ย ย ย Land more lovely sun ne'er shone on than these isles of Owhyhee,
ย ย ย ย Spendthrift Nature's wild profusion fashioned them like fairy bowers;
ย ย ย ย Yet behind--below the sweetness,--underneath the passion-flowers,
ย ย ย ย Lurked grim deeds, and things of horror, grisly Deaths, and ceaseless Fears,
ย ย ย ย Fears and Deaths that walked in Darkness, grisly Deaths and ceaseless Fears.




ย ย ย ย Mauna Loa--Mona Lo-ah.

ย ย ย ย Kilauea--Kil-o-ee-ah.

ย ย ย ย Hal๏ฟฝ-Mau-Mau--Ha-lee-Mah-oo-Mah-oo.

ย ย ย ย On the slope of Mauna Loa, in the pit of Kilauea,
ย ย ย ย In the lake of molten lava, in the sea of living fire,
ย ย ย ย In the place of Ceaseless Burnings, in her home of Wrath and Terror,
ย ย ย ย Dwelt the dreadful goddess P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ of the Lake of Fire;
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ of the place of torment, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ of the Lake of Fire.

ย ย ย ย In the dim far-off beginnings, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ flung the islands up
ย ย ย ย From the bottom of the ocean, from the darksome underworld;
ย ย ย ย Built them for a house to dwell in, built them for herself alone,
ย ย ย ย So she claimed them and their people, claimed them as her very own,
ย ย ย ย And they feared her, and they worshipped--
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, the Remorseless One.

ย ย ย ย But, at times, when she lay sleeping, underneath the lake of fire,
ย ย ย ย They forgot to do her reverence, they forgot the fiery one;
ย ย ย ย Then in wrath the goddess thundered from the Lake of Ceaseless Burnings,
ย ย ย ย Flamed and thundered in her anger, till the very skies were red,
ย ย ย ย Poured black ruin on the island, shook it to its rocky bed.

ย ย ย ย Then in fear the people trembled and bethought them of their sins,
ย ย ย ย And the great high priest of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ came like Death down Mauna Loa,
ย ย ย ย Came to soothe the awful goddess, came to choose the sacrifice,
ย ย ย ย Chose the fairest youth or maiden, pointed with a deadly finger,
ย ย ย ย Led them weeping up the mountain, victims to the Lake of Fire.

ย ย ย ย On the snowy beach of coral, youths and maidens full of laughter,
ย ย ย ย Flower-bedecked and full of laughter, sported gaily in the sun;
ย ย ย ย Up above, the slender palm-trees swung and shivered in the trade-wind,
ย ย ย ย All around them flowers and spices,--red hibiscus, sweet pandanus,
ย ย ย ย And behind, the labouring mountain groaned and growled unceasingly.

ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย "Sea and sunshine,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Care is moonshine,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย All our hearts are light with laughter.
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย We are free
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย As sun and sea,
ย ย ย ย What care we for what comes after?"

ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Bride.

ย ย ย ย "Life was sweet before Love found her,
ย ย ย ย  In his faery bowers.
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Life is sweeter,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย And completer,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Since he found her,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย There, and crowned her
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย With his fadeless flowers."

ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Bridegroom.
ย ย ย ย "Love sought long before he found her,
ย ย ย ย Ne'er was love like ours!
ย ย ย ย Long he sought her,
ย ย ย ย E'er he caught her.
ย ย ย ย But he found her
ย ย ย ย There, and bound her
ย ย ย ย With his fadeless flowers."

ย ย ย ย "Gaily sporting,
ย ย ย ย Pleasure courting,
ย ย ย ย Nought know we of care or sorrow.
ย ย ย ย We are free
ย ย ย ย As sun and sea,
ย ย ย ย What care we what comes to-morrow?"

ย ย ย ย Louder still and louder, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ roars within her lake of fire,
ย ย ย ย And the youths and maidens trembling look in fear up Mauna Loa,
ย ย ย ย Dreading sight of that grim figure stalking down the mountain side;
ย ย ย ย For when P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ claims her victims none the summons may avoid.
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ calls for whom she chooses--whom she chooses goes,--and dies.

ย ย ย ย See! He comes! They start in terror. There, along the mountain side,
ย ย ย ย Death comes stalking, slowly, surely,--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ must be satisfied.
ย ย ย ย Which among them will he summon, with his dreadful pointing finger?
ย ย ย ย All their hearts become as water, all their faces blanch with fear,
ย ย ย ย Deaths they suffer in the waiting, while dread Death draws near.

ย ย ย ย Now he stands in dreadful menace, seeking with a baleful eye
ย ย ย ย For the sweetest and the fairest--for the meetest sacrifice.
ย ย ย ย "Choose, O choose!"--they cry in terror; "choose your victim and be gone,
ย ย ย ย For we each die deaths while waiting, till dread P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ's choice be known!
ย ย ย ย Choose your victim, Priest of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, choose your victim and be gone!"

ย ย ย ย Slowly points the dreadful finger, marks the newly-wedded bride;
ย ย ย ย All the rest, save one, fall from her, as the living from the dead.
ย ย ย ย From the first of time's beginnings P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ ne'er has been gainsayed;
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ chooses whom she chooses, each and all the choice abide,
ย ย ย ย For the common good and safety,--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ must be satisfied!

ย ย ย ย Still the mountain reels and shudders, still the awful thunders peal,
ย ย ย ย Like a snake the ruthless finger holds them all in terror still;
ย ย ย ย One is there whose life is broken, parted from his chosen bride,
ย ย ย ย But the threatening finger, heedless of the lives it may divide,
ย ย ย ย Lights upon a tiny maiden,--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ must be satisfied!

ย ย ย ย Slow, the grim high-priest of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ turns to climb the mountain side;
ย ย ย ย Slow, the victims turn and follow,--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ must be satisfied.
ย ย ย ย And the rest shrink, dumb and helpless, daring not to lift an eye,
ย ย ย ย And beyond, the labouring mountain cracks and belches living fires,
ย ย ย ย Till the island reels and shudders at dread P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ's agonies.

ย ย ย ย But a greater one than P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ walked the mountain side that day;--
ย ย ย ย To them, climbing, dumb and dim-eyed--like a flash of heavenly flame,
ย ย ย ย Swift and bright as saving angel, fair Kapiolani came,
ย ย ย ย Swiftly as a saving angel, gleaming like a heavenly flame,
ย ย ย ย Thirsting like a sword for battle, fair Kapiolani came.

ย ย ย ย Radiant with the faith of martyrs, all aglow with new-born zeal,
ย ย ย ย Burning to release the people from the bondage and the thrall,
ย ย ย ย From the deadly thrall of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, from the ever-threatening doom,
ย ย ย ย From the everlasting menace, from the awful lake of fire,
ย ย ย ย Like a bright avenging angel fair Kapiolani came!

ย ย ย ย "Hear me now, you priest of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, and ye men of Owhyhee!
ย ย ย ย Hearken! ye who cringe and tremble, at the sound of Kilauea,
ย ย ย ย Fearful of the wrath of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, fearful of the lake of fire!--
ย ย ย ย Priest, I say there is no P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ is not--never was!
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ lives but in your legends--there is only one true God!"

ย ย ย ย "Curs๏ฟฝd, thrice accurs๏ฟฝd, you who thus great P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ do defy,
ย ย ย ย Here, upon her sacred mountain, of a surety you shall die!
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, mighty P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, Vengeance! Strike her with thy dreadful doom!
ย ย ย ย So let every scoffer perish!--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! come!"
ย ย ย ย And Kapiolani answered--"P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! come!"

ย ย ย ย Loud the mountain roared and thundered; shuddered all who heard and saw,
ย ย ย ย Dauntless stood Kapiolani, dauntless with her faithful few.
ย ย ย ย "Come!" she cried again. "Come, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! Smite me with thy dreadful doom!
ย ย ย ย I am waiting, mighty P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ!--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! come!"
ย ย ย ย And the mountain roared and thundered;--but the goddess did not come.

ย ย ย ย "Hearken, Priest! You have deceived us. All your life has been a lie,
ย ย ย ย Black your heart is, red your hands are, with the blood of those who die.
ย ย ย ย All these years you have misled us with your awful threats of doom.
ย ย ย ย Now it ends! I do defy you, and your goddess I defy.
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, is not, never has been. All your worship is a lie.

ย ย ย ย "I will climb your sacred mountain. I will dare your lake of fire.
ย ย ย ย I will eat your sacred berries. I will dare your goddess there,
ย ย ย ย There and then to wreak her vengeance, then and there to come in fire,
ย ย ย ย And with awful burnings end me, now and for eternity;
ย ย ย ย But if P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ does not end me, then her worship ends this day."

ย ย ย ย Then the great high priest of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ turned to fiery Kilauea.
ย ย ย ย "Come!" he said, "the goddess calls you!"--and they climbed the mountain side,
ย ย ย ย Up the slopes of Mauna Loa, to the hell of Kilauea,
ย ย ย ย With the bright blue sky above them, with the blazing sun above them,
ย ย ย ย While the mountain shook beneath them, and its head was wrapped in fire.

ย ย ย ย Fearful, hopeful, all the people crept along the shaking path,
ย ย ย ย Hardly breathing at their daring, thus to brave dread P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ's wrath,
ย ย ย ย Bending low lest she should see them, breathing soft lest she should hear,
ย ย ย ย Certain that Kapiolani would be sacrificed that day,
ย ย ย ย To the vengeance of the goddess, to the anger of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ.

ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย "As little child
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย On mother's breast,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย O rest, my heart,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Have rest!
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Who rests on Him
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Is surely blest.
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย So rest, my heart,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Have rest!
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย As warrior bold
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย His foes among,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Be strong, my heart,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Be strong!
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Who rests on Him
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Shall ne'er go wrong.
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Be strong, my heart,
ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย ย Be strong!"

ย ย ย ย Thus, Kapiolani, dauntless, singing softly as she went,
ย ย ย ย With a face as calm and fearless as a child on pleasure bent,
ย ย ย ย Climbed the side of Mauna Loa, to the dreadful lake of fire,
ย ย ย ย While the mountain shook and thundered, while the people blanched and shuddered,
ย ย ย ย Climbed to Hal๏ฟฝ-Mau-Mau,--to the dreadful lake of fire.

ย ย ย ย All the people waited trembling, stood afar off pale and trembling,
ย ย ย ย While Kapiolani, fearless, climbed up to the lake of fire,
ย ย ย ย With the fiery glow all round her, with a heavenly light about her.
ย ย ย ย Shining with a radiance brighter than since time began had shone
ย ย ย ย From the Lake of Ceaseless Burnings, from the dreadful lake of fire.

ย ย ย ย "Here," she cried, "I pluck your berries, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ,--and I give you none!
ย ย ย ย See! I eat your sacred berries, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ,--and I give you none!
ย ย ย ย P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, here I break your tabus! Come, with all your dreadful fires!
ย ย ย ย Burn me, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! I defy you!--P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! come!"
ย ย ย ย Come now, P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ, or for ever own that you are overcome!

ย ย ย ย "P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ comes not. Is she sleeping? Is she wandering to-day?
ย ย ย ย Is she busy with her burnings? Has the goddess nought to say?
ย ย ย ย Hear me, friends!--There is no P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ! One true God alone there is.
ย ย ย ย His, this mountain! His, these burnings! You, and I, and all things,--His!
ย ย ย ย Goodness, Mercy, Loving-Kindness, Life Eternal--all are His!

ย ย ย ย "From this day, let no man tremble, when he feels the mountain shake!
ย ย ย ย From this day, no man or maiden shall be killed for P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ's sake!
ย ย ย ย From this day, we break the thraldom of the dreadful lake of fire.
ย ย ย ย From this day, we pass for ever from the scourge of P๏ฟฝl๏ฟฝ's rod.--
ย ย ย ย From this day, Thou, Lord Jehovah, be our one and only God!"
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Arthur Dunkerley (John Oxenham), titled "Kapiolani", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
